Inkar Data Wrangling and Descriptives

Finn_Krüger

2023-11-09

Inkar Main Data

Loading and data wrangling

INKAR <- read.csv('Inkar_finale.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ";")

Citizens <- read.csv('Einwohner_Bundesländer.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ";")

BIP <- read.csv('BIP.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ";")

INKAR <- INKAR %>%
  left_join(Citizens, join_by(Kennziffer, Aggregat, Raumeinheit)) %>%
  left_join(BIP, join_by(Kennziffer, Aggregat, Raumeinheit))

names(INKAR)[-(1:3)]<-paste(gsub("\\.[0-9]{1,2}$","",names(INKAR)[-(1:3)]),
                            as.character(INKAR[1,-(1:3)]), sep="_")
INKAR <- INKAR[-1,]

INKAR <- pivot_longer(INKAR, -c(Kennziffer, Raumeinheit, Aggregat), names_sep="_", names_to=c(".value", "year")) 

#### Replacing kommas with dots, make these columns numeric 

comma_cols <- sapply(INKAR, function(x) any(grepl(",", x, fixed = TRUE)))

colnames(INKAR)
##  [1] "Kennziffer"                                 
##  [2] "Raumeinheit"                                
##  [3] "Aggregat"                                   
##  [4] "year"                                       
##  [5] "Arbeitslosenquote"                          
##  [6] "Erwerbstätige.Primärer.Sektor"              
##  [7] "Erwerbstätige.Sekundärer.Sektor"            
##  [8] "Erwerbstätige.Tertiärer.Sektor"             
##  [9] "Ausgaben.für.Sachinvestitionen"             
## [10] "Zuweisungen.für.Investitionsfördermaßnahmen"
## [11] "Asylbewerber"                               
## [12] "SGBII.Leistungen"                           
## [13] "Leistungen.für.Wohngeld"                    
## [14] "Haushaltseinkommen"                         
## [15] "Haushalte.mit.niedrigem.Einkommen"          
## [16] "Haushalte.mit.mittlerem.Einkommen"          
## [17] "Haushalte.mit.hohem.Einkommen"              
## [18] "Bruttoinlandsprodukt.je.Einwohner"          
## [19] "Ausländeranteil"                            
## [20] "Industriequote"                             
## [21] "Bevölkerung.gesamt"                         
## [22] "Bruttoinlandsprodukt.in.1000.Euro"
INKAR[, comma_cols] <- lapply(INKAR[, comma_cols], function(x) gsub(".", "", x, fixed = TRUE))
INKAR[, comma_cols] <- lapply(INKAR[, comma_cols], function(x) gsub(",", ".", x, fixed = TRUE))
INKAR[, comma_cols] <- lapply(INKAR[, comma_cols], as.numeric)


INKAR <- INKAR %>%
  filter(year > 2008) %>%
  mutate(year = as.numeric(year),
          poor_over_rich_HH = Haushalte.mit.niedrigem.Einkommen / Haushalte.mit.hohem.Einkommen)

colnames(INKAR)
##  [1] "Kennziffer"                                 
##  [2] "Raumeinheit"                                
##  [3] "Aggregat"                                   
##  [4] "year"                                       
##  [5] "Arbeitslosenquote"

##  [7] "Erwerbstätige.Sekundärer.Sektor"            
##  [8] "Erwerbstätige.Tertiärer.Sektor"

Social Spending Data from Destatis

### Does not contain Grundsicherung im Alter und bei Erwerbsminderung & Hilfe z.Üb.bes.soz.Schwierigk.u.in and.Lebenslagen - Retrieved from DE STATIS Social_Spending <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/DESTATIS/Social_Spending_per_Region/22111-0022_de_flat.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ";")

colnames(Social_Spending)
##  [1] "Statistik_Code"                   "Statistik_Label"                 
##  [3] "Zeit_Code"                        "Zeit_Label"                      
##  [5] "Zeit"                             "X1_Merkmal_Code"                 
##  [7] "X1_Merkmal_Label"                 "X1_Auspraegung_Code"             
##  [9] "X1_Auspraegung_Label"             "X2_Merkmal_Code"                 
## [11] "X2_Merkmal_Label"                 "X2_Auspraegung_Code"             
## [13] "X2_Auspraegung_Label"             "BAG001__Bruttoausgaben__Tsd._EUR"
## [15] "BAG001__Bruttoausgaben__q"        "ENM001__Bruttoausgaben__Tsd._EUR"
## [17] "ENM001__Bruttoausgaben__q"        "ASG010__Einnahmen__Tsd._EUR"     
## [19] "ASG010__Einnahmen__q"
Social_Spending_1 <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/DESTATIS/Social_Spending_per_Region/22111-0022_de_flat.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ";") %>%
  rename(Kennziffer = X1_Auspraegung_Code,
         year = Zeit)%>%
  group_by(Kennziffer, year)%>%
  summarise(Social_Expenditure_without = sum(ASG010__Einnahmen__Tsd._EUR))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Kennziffer'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.

Merge Data and make Regional Database

Regional_Statistics <- INKAR %>%
  left_join(Social_Spending_1, join_by(Kennziffer, year))


Regional_Statistics <- Regional_Statistics %>%
  mutate( Social_Spending_per_GDP = (Social_Expenditure_without*10000)/      (Bruttoinlandsprodukt.in.1000.Euro*1000))

# keep only variables of interest 

Regional_Statistics <- Regional_Statistics %>%
  select(Kennziffer, Raumeinheit, year, Bevölkerung.gesamt, Arbeitslosenquote, Asylbewerber, Haushaltseinkommen, Haushalte.mit.niedrigem.Einkommen,Haushalte.mit.mittlerem.Einkommen, Haushalte.mit.hohem.Einkommen, Bruttoinlandsprodukt.je.Einwohner, Ausländeranteil, Bruttoinlandsprodukt.in.1000.Euro, poor_over_rich_HH, Social_Expenditure_without, Social_Spending_per_GDP)


Check_Social  <- Regional_Statistics %>%
  select(Raumeinheit, year, Social_Spending_per_GDP)

Regional_Statistics[-(1:3)] <- lapply(Regional_Statistics[-(1:3)], as.numeric)

Regional_Statistics <- Regional_Statistics %>%
  filter(year > 2009)

Creating variables of interest

Changes in

### Function that creates lags for variables

calculate_lagged_differences <- function(data, variable_name, num_lags) {
  for (lag in 1:num_lags) {
    lagged_col <- paste0(variable_name, "_lag_", lag)
    data[[lagged_col]] <- c(rep(NA, lag), diff(data[[variable_name]], lag = lag))
  }
  return(data)
}

# Example usage:
columns <- c('Social_Spending_per_GDP')
num_lags <- 3

for (col in columns) {
  Regional_Statistics <- calculate_lagged_differences(Regional_Statistics, col, num_lags)
}

Descriptives

Checking Summary Statistics

### checking all variables 


for (i in colnames(Regional_Statistics)) {
  print(i)
  print(class(i))
  print(summary(Regional_Statistics[, i]))
  }

Descriptives

distributions

colnames(Regional_Statistics)
##  [1] "Kennziffer"                        "Raumeinheit"                      
##  [3] "year"                              "Bevölkerung.gesamt"               
##  [5] "Arbeitslosenquote"                 "Asylbewerber"                     
##  [7] "Haushaltseinkommen"                "Haushalte.mit.niedrigem.Einkommen"
##  [9] "Haushalte.mit.mittlerem.Einkommen" "Haushalte.mit.hohem.Einkommen"    
## [11] "Bruttoinlandsprodukt.je.Einwohner" "Ausländeranteil"                  
## [13] "Bruttoinlandsprodukt.in.1000.Euro" "poor_over_rich_HH"                
## [15] "Social_Expenditure_without"        "Social_Spending_per_GDP"          
## [17] "Social_Spending_per_GDP_lag_1"     "Social_Spending_per_GDP_lag_2"    
## [19] "Social_Spending_per_GDP_lag_3"
variables_of_interest <- c('Arbeitslosenquote', "Haushaltseinkommen", "Asylbewerber", "Ausländeranteil", 
               "Bruttoinlandsprodukt.je.Einwohner", "poor_over_rich_HH", 
               "Social_Spending_per_GDP")

for (variable in variables_of_interest) {
  plot <- ggplot(Regional_Statistics, aes(x = get(variable), fill = factor(year))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = paste("Distribution of", variable),
         x = variable,
         y = "Density") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  print(plot)
}
